rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. HAN ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. The judgment debtor claims that the judgment debtor does not actually owe the money the subject of the judgment. That claim cannot be adjudicated in this proceeding regarding claims of exemption. The judgment debtor does not provide authority for the proposition that timing issues noted in the judgment debtor's "motion to deny" suffices to cancel the present proceeding. The judgment debtor can afford $80 per pay period ($160 per month) and the claim of exemption is denied to that extent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK KELLY VS. THOMAS WHITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses; Memo P/A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 24, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PATRICK KELLY'S Motion To Compel Responses; Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED without prejudice. Movant did not provide sufficient notice of the hearing date as required by C.C.P. 1005(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: GENERAL CHARLES E. CHUCK YEAGER VS. VIRGIN AMERICA, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT VIRGIN AMERICA, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's motion To Enforce Settlement. Denied, without prejudice. The court cannot determine the merits of the motion because the envelope lodged conditionally under seal did not contain un-redacted copies of the two settlement agreements at issue, but rather redacted copies. (Baldwin Declaration, Exhibits A, B.) See California Rule of Court 2.551(b)(3)(A)(i) & (5). Further, Virgin America improperly filed additional evidence in reply addressing the merits of the opposition. The motion should be re-filed. The parties are reminded that only the minimum amount of content should be proposed for sealing, and that generally the sealing of new documents requires a separate motion. Judge Karnow discloses that he has purchased an option for personal transport from a corporation affiliated with Virgin America, i.e., Virgin Galactic. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY E. HELD PHD VS. HOT TOPIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Approve Prop 65 Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2012, Line 17, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD'S Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement. Denied. The evidentiary basis for the attorneys fees claimed is insufficient. The claims regarding actual fees incurred (over $50,000) are exorbitant. From the materials presented to the court it does not appear that fees in excess of $25,000 are supportable. The Court notes the remarkable similarly of certain portions of the fees claimed here to those claimed in another matter on today's calendar, JOHN MOORE VS. IN GEAR FASHIONS. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN MOORE VS. TRADE ASSOCIATES GROUP, LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2012, Line 18, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent. Denied. The civil penalty of $1750 is trivial and has no deterrent effect. While the claims regarding actually incurred fees (over $60,000) are exorbitant, the sums for which movant seeks approval appear reasonable. Counsel signing the declaration in support of the attorneys' fees (Sayad) must present and file a declaration under penalty of perjury that all sums listed under Statement of Fee and Costs (Ex B) were all actually incurred in connection with this and no other action, and none is claimed or will be claimed in connection with any other motion for court approval. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: SAHIL MANSURI VS. LAUREN MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Deft And Req. For Monetary Sanctions And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 24, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SAHIL MANSURI Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Deft And Req. For Monetary SanctionsPro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff propounded Judicial Council Form Interrogatories- General (DISC-001) to each defendant. Plaintiff checked the box for 19 of these Form Interrogatories in each set. Plaintiff also propounded 16 identical requests for production to each defendant. Each defendant answered the first 16 interrogatories in each set (including all of their subparts), and then objected to the remaining interrogatories and all of the document requests on the ground that, "[t]he propounding party has exceeded the permissible limit of 35 interrogatories." None of the defendants objected to any of the interrogatories on the ground that they contained subparts. This would have been a valid objection to each interrogatory containing subparts. C.C.P. Section 94(a) allows a party in a limited civil case to propound to each adverse party, "any combination of 35 of the following:(1) Interrogatories (with no subparts) . . . Demands to produce documents or things . . . and (3) Requests for admission (with no subparts) . . . ." Use of the Form Interrogatories-General in limited civil litigation violates the rule against propounding interrogatories with subparts in Section 94(a), and the Judicial Council has promulgated Form Interrogatories for use in limited civil cases that do not contain subparts. See Judicial Council Form Interrogatories - Limited Civil Cases (Economic Litigation) form DISC-004. However, by answering without failing to object to the interrogatories containing subparts, the defendants waived their objections. Nonetheless the court will not order the defendants to answer additional interrogatories that violate C.C.P. Section 94(a). Each Defendant shall respond to the 16 requests for production of documents in Plaintiff's Requests for Production - Set One. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CURTIS KEKOA JR. VS. GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANT GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. The complaint pleads sufficient facts to support the prayer for punitive damages. See e.g. Complaint 34, 36. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CURTIS KEKOA JR. VS. GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Geico General Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2012, Line 22, Geico General Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Complaint. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Relying at this time solely on the language of the insurance policy, it appears that its coverage of disputes arising out of the "ownership" of the vehicle applies, as it appears the protagonists in the underlying suit were engaged in a dispute concerning that ownership. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
